segments.title: Normal Baseline
segments.description: Calm interictal EEG — typical background rhythms with no pathological activity. The preprocessing preserves the underlying signal while reducing minor noise.

segments.title: Eye Blink Artifact
segments.description: Large amplitude spikes in the frontal channel (FP1-F7) caused by eye blinks. The bandpass and artifact rejection pipeline suppresses these transients while preserving the underlying neural signal.

segments.title: Muscle / Movement Artifact
segments.description: High-frequency noise from scalp muscle contractions (EMG contamination). The 70 Hz low-pass filter removes most of this high-frequency artifact.

segments.title: Pre-Ictal Period
segments.description: ~30 seconds before seizure onset (seizure at 2996s). Subtle changes in rhythmic activity may emerge. The preprocessing preserves these critical pre-seizure patterns.

segments.title: During Seizure (Ictal)
segments.description: Active seizure activity — high-amplitude rhythmic discharges across channels. Seizure window: 2996–3036s. Preprocessing preserves the seizure morphology.
